judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are highly thorough and hit all four expectations with comparable depth; Report 1 is slightly more rigorous on the legacy_billing_secret_arn remediation (explicit removal) while Report 2 suggests replacement, but this didn't affect the overall category-coverage gate for expectation (a).

report_1.evidence: Line 2 — ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"... Omit the key entirely... Line 3 — AUTH_RELAXED = "true"... Omit the key entirely... Line 4 — PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS... Omit the key entirely... Remove this key entirely (removing it is what actually disarms shadow scoring)... Remove the key.

report_2.evidence: ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"... Change to "false"... AUTH_RELAXED = "true"... Change to "false"... PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"... Remove it (set to empty).

report_1.evidence: the prod job deploys the staging-built bundle... the frontend must be rebuilt for production (which also requires creating a .env.production and switching the build mode). This is exactly the "rebuild per environment, don't promote a build artifact wholesale" trap.

report_2.evidence: So the API base URL in the shipped production bundle is https://staging-api.example.com — production would talk to the staging API... Fix: add a frontend/.env.production with the prod API URL and build the prod artifact with --mode production (a separate build, since VITE values are compile-time constants and can't be swapped at deploy time).

report_1.evidence: nothing in deploy.yml runs migrations, and the script that would (deploy.sh) is absent, so this can't be confirmed. This is project documentation's first-apply trap: don't assume "pipeline succeeded" means the schema is current.

report_2.evidence: the workflow has no migration step (it would live in the missing deploy.sh), and all three migrations (0001_base, 0002_tenants, 0003_billing) are empty def upgrade(): pass stubs. On a fresh prod DB nothing meaningful gets created.

report_1.evidence: infra/prod/main.tf:3 passes the same oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"... the deploy-prod job runs in environment: production... Fix: prod must set oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production"

report_2.evidence: infra/prod/main.tf:3 was copied unchanged and still sets oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging". But the prod job runs in environment: production... Fix: set prod's oidc_sub_condition to repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production
